commit.message:
Move silhouette/glow to outer container, anchor tower to bottom

- SVG silhouette and radial glow now position relative to full
  container (centered on screen) instead of the rows div
- justify-end pb-8: tower grows upward from the bottom
- Dynamic --rise-dist per row for foundation-up animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Anchor tower to bottom so words build up from foundation

justify-end pb-8: single word sits at screen bottom, subsequent
words stack upward — visually builds from the ground up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dynamic rise distance: each row rises from proportional depth below base

Bottom row rises 40px, top row rises (N-1)*50+40px — words physically
travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase towerRowIn translateY for more dramatic rise from base

60px travel distance makes each row visibly rise up from below
instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix word placement to build tower from base

Iterate placement loop bottom→top so base rows (wide, appear first in
animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

feat: FLOW Card v1.6 — compression-first product architecture

The Living Card is now the primary artifact:
- FlowCard schema with OÙ/QUAND/QUOI opportunities
- /api/flow/state returns { card, depth? } — card always, depth for radar
- Per-opportunity TTL enforcement (silence > stale)

Key features:
- kind: 'confirme' | 'piste' — deterministic vs pattern bet
- Vocabulary firewall: bans signal, ramification, saturation, etc. from UI
- Generic skeleton filter: drops vague patterns like "structure mercredi"
- Hash-based journal dedup: write only when hash changes or 15-min heartbeat

New files:
- src/types/flow-card.ts: FlowCard, Opportunity, cardToWhatsApp, cardToHUD
- src/lib/flow-engine/card-emitter.ts: buildFlowCard, TTL, vocabulary sanitizer
- data/registry/magnets.paris.json: 15 Paris zones + corridors
- supabase/migrations/002_card_journal.sql: journal, feedback, shifts tables

Tests verify:
- TTL expiration at 04:21 drops "bars pleins" when window ended at 02:30
- READ mode shows only action/place/why/window
- WhatsApp equals compressed card one-liner
- Banned vocabulary removed from driver-facing text

commit.message:
Add Mo & Conn (2018) paper screenshots for 2026-03-09 lecture

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stuck detection: fire on time alone, not on transfer ID presence

Stuck detection was gated on _transfer_id is not None, meaning if the
filename comparison failed for any reason, the 10-minute timeout never
fired and the download would hang for the full 60-minute loop.

Now the time check runs unconditionally (just needs completed=0, failed=0).
If _transfer_id is available, we also cancel the slskd transfer; otherwise
we just mark as stuck and return. Same fix applied to both album and track
poll functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix audio dropping after ~11s on audio-only files

schedule_buffer was silently discarding samples when the ring buffer
was full. For audio-only files the decoder outruns real-time playback,
so every sample past ~11s (ring capacity) was lost. Retry with 1ms
backoff until all samples are pushed.

Also fix all clippy warnings surfaced by newer Rust toolchain.

commit.message:
feat: agent runtime security execution environment (#19104)

Implements the requested patch-first subsumption plan for "OpenSandbox"
execution environments in Summit.

### Implementation Checklist
- [x] Defined interfaces: `AgentExecutionRuntime`, `ToolInvocationGate`,
`SandboxManager`, `ExecutionAuditLogger`
- [x] Implemented core runtime module (`BaseAgentRuntime`) to mediate
tool calls and sandbox execution
- [x] Added `DockerSandboxManager` for isolated runtime containers
- [x] Implemented `YamlToolPolicyGate` and associated policy file
`.github/policies/agent-runtime/tool_access_policy.yaml`
- [x] Added `ConsoleAuditLogger` with "never-log" sensitive data
redaction for audit trails
- [x] Added CI verifier pipeline:
`.github/workflows/ci-agent-runtime.yml` with shim job linking to
ADR-052
- [x] Added `.github/required_checks.todo.md` for later GitHub API
compliance discovery

### Testing
Ran typescript checks (`tsc --noEmit`), linters, and the CI workflow is
structured as specified in the subsumption task.

commit.message:
Fix tabs, variable insertion, and panel wiring

Root cause: using document.getElementById() risks colliding with Gmail's
own DOM IDs. Replaced all inner element lookups with data-gsid attributes
queried only within #gsendr-panel, guaranteeing the right element.

Other fixes:
- Split one massive template literal into small builder functions —
  eliminates parse errors, easier to debug
- All onclick handlers wrapped in try/catch with console.warn
- Variable tags show brief blue flash on click to confirm insertion
- Tabs properly switch panes via scoped querySelector
- Sequence step fields (subj/delay/cond/body) fully synced
- "Sending to" section: shows detected recipient + Import CSV link
  that opens the dashboard shows page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Decentralized Infrastructure Delivery Framework (DIDF) (#19103)

This PR introduces the Decentralized Infrastructure Delivery Framework
(DIDF), subsumed from the Adidas use case.

It implements the following foundational patterns:
- A new `InfraModule` registry interface in `src/platform/infra`.
- Policy enforcement templates using OPA Rego (`resource_naming`,
`environment_scope`, `dependency_allowedlist`, `cost_guardrails`).
- A CI workflow skeleton (`infra-verify.yml`) that validates modules,
applies policies, and acts as a dry-run gateway.
- Foundational architectural and governance documentation detailing the
decentralized pattern, stack compositions, and governance models.
- Evidence structures for the infrastructure module audits
(`report.json`, `metrics.json`, `stamp.json`), integrated into the
unified evidence ledger (`evidence/index.json` under
`EVD-ADIDAS-IAC-001`).

Tests were run locally to ensure no pre-existing breakages were
introduced. No front-end logic was altered.

commit.message:
fix(portal): migrate auth+invoices routes to adminClient for RLS safety [Story 18.1.1]

Portal auth POST and invoices GET used createClient() (user RLS) which
depended on bypass policies. Migrated to adminClient with explicit
client_id/org_id scoping as prerequisite for dropping bypass policies.

- auth/route.ts: portal user lookup, update, activity insert -> adminClient
- invoices/route.ts: invoices query, integrations query -> adminClient + org_id scope

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: break infinite commission_progress event loop between toolbox and lifecycle (#66)

The toolbox emits commission_progress/question/result to the EventBus,
which the orchestrator's subscription routes into lifecycle methods.
Those lifecycle methods returned a deferredEvent that withLock() re-emitted
to the EventBus, creating an infinite synchronous recursion. The result
event was masked by a resultSignalReceived guard, but progress and question
events looped without bound.

Remove deferredEvent from progressReported, resultSubmitted, and
questionLogged since they are only called from the EventBus subscription
path where the event is already published.

Co-authored-by: Test <test@test.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(mvcc,pager,vdbe): filter agreeing pages from sheaf obstructions, zero freelist trunk pages, and fix coroutine test

**MVCC Sheaf Conformal Analysis (sheaf_conformal.rs)**
- Fixed false-positive sheaf obstructions: overlapping pages where both
  transactions agree on the version are no longer reported as obstructions
- `check_sheaf_consistency()`: uses version_order function to test agreement
  when available, falls back to equality check otherwise
- `check_sheaf_consistency_with_chains()`: same fix for chain-based analysis
- `OpportunityScore::score()`: clamp effort to f64::EPSILON minimum to prevent
  INFINITY scores (which would always pass the gate) when effort is zero/negative

**Pager Freelist Serialization (pager.rs)**
- Zero entire freelist trunk page buffer before writing to prevent leaking
  stale data from the page pool in the unused tail of trunk pages

**VDBE Codegen (codegen.rs)**
- Removed redundant Literal/Placeholder match arm in emit_upsert_expr (now
  handled by wildcard arm, avoiding dead code)
- Fixed INSERT test assertions: InsertSource::Values now provides correct
  number of placeholder values matching table column count

**VDBE Engine — Coroutine test rewrite (engine.rs)**
- Rewrote InitCoroutine/Yield test to use correct producer-consumer structure:
  consumer emits Yield+ResultRow pairs, producer emits Integer+Yield pairs
- Uses `op_at_mut()` to patch InitCoroutine p2/p3 after both sections are
  emitted, avoiding forward-reference label resolution issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

?include_related=["trigger","job"].

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restrict XP awards — no XP for casual observation

GM was awarding XP for looking at noticeboards, shrines, and trivial
actions by interpreting "first-time discoveries" too broadly.

New rules:
- XP only for genuine achievement: quests, combat, solved mysteries,
  major social victories, first arrival at significant new locations
- Explicit DO NOT list: looking around, reading signs, examining objects,
  casual conversation, routine actions
- Calibrated amounts: weak combat 10-20, quests 50-150, etc.
- Rule of thumb: when in doubt, give no XP

commit.message:
feat: add CI pipeline, comprehensive benchmarks, TUI snapshot tests, and expand model/analysis/robot surfaces

**CI Pipeline (.github/workflows/ci.yml)**
- New GitHub Actions workflow with cargo check, clippy, fmt, and test
  steps across stable and nightly Rust toolchains

**Benchmarks — comprehensive rewrite (benches/triage.rs, +270 lines)**
- Replaced single-function benchmark with 11 criterion groups covering
  analyzer construction, triage, insights, plan, diff, forecast, suggest,
  alerts, history, cycle detection, and real-fixture performance
- Added synthetic fixture generators: sparse (linear chain), dense (~3
  deps/issue), and cyclic (mutual deps every 10 issues) at 100/500/1000 scale
- Each group tests across graph topologies for scaling characterization

**TUI Snapshot Tests (src/snapshots/, src/tui.rs, +1400 lines)**
- Added 20 insta snapshot files covering main/board/insights/graph/history
  views at narrow/medium/wide breakpoints
- Snapshot tests for modal overlays (help, quit confirm), search filtering,
  insights panel cycling, and history git mode
- TUI rendering infrastructure expanded to support non-interactive snapshot
  capture with deterministic fixture data

**Model Expansion (src/model.rs, +317 lines)**
- Extended Issue struct with new fields for enhanced triage scoring
- Added Default derive and helper methods for synthetic issue generation
- Dependency struct extended with default support

**Analysis Extensions**
- diff.rs: +158 lines of new tests — empty snapshots, all-new, all-removed,
  identical, priority change, status change, reopened, label diff, dependency
  changes, and concurrent add/remove scenarios
- graph.rs: +99 lines — additional graph analysis utilities and edge cases
- triage.rs: +238 lines — expanded triage scoring and recommendation logic

**Robot Output (src/robot.rs, +104 lines)**
- Extended robot JSON output with additional envelope fields and metadata

**CLI (src/cli.rs, +9 lines)**
- Added new CLI flags for snapshot testing and debug rendering modes

**Error Handling (src/error.rs, +58 lines)**
- New error variants for snapshot and rendering failures

**Documentation**
- FEATURE_PARITY.md: updated parity status for 20+ flags across all waves
- PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md: refined architecture description
- README.md: expanded usage examples and feature documentation

**Beads Issue Tracker**
- Updated .beads/issues.jsonl with current status
- Added .br_history snapshots for audit trail

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Security: Fix mass assignment, input validation, and remaining vulnerabilities

Mass Assignment Prevention (added field whitelists):
- workflows/service.py: Whitelist for workflow updates
- teams/service.py: Whitelist for team updates
- profiles/service.py: Whitelist for prompt updates
- tenants/routes.py: Whitelist for tenant settings updates
- marketplace/service.py: Whitelist for listing updates

Input Validation:
- cli/main.py: Validate alembic revision format (prevent injection)
- workflows/routes.py: Add timeout_seconds range (1-3600), limit range (1-500)
- devices/routes.py: Add timeout_seconds range (1-300)
- queues/routes.py: Add validation for max_size, retries, timeout, priority, delay, ttl
- phone/routes.py: Add validation for max_call_duration, silence_timeout
- storage/routes.py: Add file type validation (block executables, detect scripts)

Cryptographic Improvements:
- security/encryption.py: Add key strength validation and weak key warnings

commit.message:
fix(mine): reset submissionStatus on each task cycle + show task progress when equation solved

- useCompute.ts: reset submissionStatus to "idle" at the top of each
  mining loop iteration so the verified/awaiting/failed badge clears
  before the next task begins
- MissionControl.tsx: when equation is solved but block tasks are still
  running, show "SOLVED ✓ — X/20 tasks" with a pulsing bar instead of
  a static solid green, so users know mining is still active
- blocks/page.tsx: move time-dependent values (blockHeight, reward,
  distance) into a useEffect to fix hydration mismatch; add 15s timeout
  to both fetch calls
- leaderboard/page.tsx: add 15s AbortSignal timeout to fetch
